body.title: Migrate wild_igor into devices/igor/ — Igor becomes an ADC device
body.result: design complete — spawned T-igor-subtree-import, T-igor-import-rewrite, T-igor-theigers-archive (D-igor-into-adc-2026-05-25)
body.description:
Igor's cognition engine (wild_igor/) currently lives in a separate repo (~/TheIgors) and the ADC has a hard rule against importing it. This inverts the correct dependency direction: Igor is a device on the rack, not the host for everything. Migrating wild_igor/ into devices/igor/ makes the ADC the primary repo and Igor a peer device alongside librarian, inference, etc.

People should be able to install and run the ADC without Igor. Igor should not be able to run without the ADC platform.

**Migration path (to design in sprint):**
1. Copy wild_igor/ → devices/igor/ with git history preserved (git subtree or filter-branch)
2. Update import root: wild_igor.igor.* → devices.igor.* (or igor.* if device is own package)
3. Update igor/shim.py in ADC to reflect new layout
4. lab/utility_closet/ dissolves: budget.py → devices/inference/ (done), db_proxy.py → platform/, registry.py stays in skeleton/ (future platform/)
5. TheIgors repo becomes ~/TheIgorsProject — history/docs archive only (lab/design_docs/, decisions_log, slate archive, lab/claudecode/ skills)

**Affected files:** devices/igor/ (new — all of wild_igor/), agent_datacenter/devices/igor/shim.py, lab/utility_closet/ (gutted), pyproject.toml (consolidated deps)
**Design rules:** no-cross-device-imports (replaces no-TheIgors-imports); platform/ is the only shared import target for devices
**Scope boundary:** Igor's Postgres schema and IGOR_HOME_DB_URL stay unchanged. DB migration history stays. Skills/habits in DB stay. Only the *code location* changes. ~/TheIgorsProject archive is a separate step.
**Test plan:** Igor's existing test suite passes from new location. ADC full suite passes. Igor starts and connects to rack after migration. Existing MCP tools work.
